input.instruction: A weakener fired on a credibility evidence package. Using ONLY the supplied pattern definition and measures, explain what the flag means, why it fired, and what the engineer should do. Choose ONE action_class from the §5B vocabulary, report a verbalized confidence, and escalate if uncertain. Stay within measure-don't-judge.

fired_pattern.definition: Benchmark coverage gap: solution-verification evidence does not span the application domain. The surrogate's residual-against-reference checks were performed only at the trained discretization/parameter regime, so verification does not cover the resolution or parameter range the COU evaluates at.
fired_pattern.standard_anchor: ASME V&V 40-2018 verification; UofA surrogate proto-taxonomy D-VER-06 (solution verification / benchmark coverage), keyed to Jakeman et al. (arXiv:2502.15496)

measures.spectral_residual_high_band: elevated at the evaluation resolution, not characterized in the verification set

case_context.cou: Fourier neural operator surrogate predicts a turbulence field used to size a mixing component; fine-scale structure contributes to the quantity of interest
case_context.decision_consequence: component sizing carried forward on the surrogate field

answer_key.gold_mechanism: Solution-verification covered only the trained 64x64 grid and viscosity band; the COU evaluates at 256x256 and lower viscosity. FNOs exhibit spectral bias — they capture large-scale components but miss new fine-scale oscillations at finer grids — and degrade under parameter extrapolation, so the verification benchmark does not cover the regime where the prediction is used.

parameters.scope: restrict the COU to the verified 64x64 grid and viscosity band [1e-3, 1e-2]

forbidden_claims: asserts the surrogate's fine-scale prediction is wrong (a correctness verdict the flag does not support)
treats in-band verification error as evidence of accuracy at the finer grid / lower viscosity
recommends accepting the 256x256 field on the basis of the 64x64 verification

label_provenance.external_grounding: Failure modes of Fourier Neural Operators (arXiv:2601.11428): FNOs predict large-scale components well but fail to capture new fine-scale oscillations on finer grids (spectral bias), and parameter extrapolation beyond the training range degrades. OOD generalization of deep-learning PDE surrogates in the small-data regime (arXiv:2601.08404).

by_field.gold_mechanism: paper-grounded (FNO spectral bias + parameter extrapolation) + the declared verification/evaluation regimes
by_field.gold_action.selected_class: provisional-self-adjudicated (restrict-cou vs acquire-validation vs supply-evidence all coherent; restrict-cou chosen as the mechanically-simple disposition that keeps the credibility claim inside the verified regime)
